name: Scrub Daddy
tagline: The smiley-face sponge that changes texture with water temperature
description: Scrub Daddy is a smiley-face shaped sponge that becomes firm in cold water for tough scrubbing and soft in warm water for gentle cleaning. It's odor-resistant, scratch-free on delicate surfaces, and one of the best-selling products in Shark Tank history.
currentStatus: Still thriving — over $900M in lifetime sales

name: Bombas
tagline: The most comfortable socks ever made — buy one, donate one
description: Bombas makes premium athletic socks engineered for comfort. For every pair sold, they donate a pair to homeless shelters. They've donated over 100 million items and generate hundreds of millions in annual revenue.
currentStatus: One of Shark Tank's greatest success stories — $300M+ annual revenue

name: Ring (Doorbot)
tagline: Video doorbell that lets you see and speak to visitors from anywhere
description: Ring started as 'Doorbot' — a Wi-Fi enabled video doorbell. Despite all sharks declining, Ring grew to dominate the smart home security market and was acquired by Amazon for over $1 billion.
currentStatus: Acquired by Amazon for $1B+ — biggest 'no deal' success in Shark Tank history

name: Squatty Potty
tagline: The toilet stool that changes the way you go
description: Squatty Potty is a bathroom stool designed to elevate your feet while sitting on the toilet, mimicking a natural squatting position. Famous for its viral unicorn commercial.
currentStatus: Went viral after Shark Tank — $33M in sales within a year

name: Cousins Maine Lobster
tagline: Fresh Maine lobster rolls served from food trucks across America
description: Two cousins from Maine started a food truck serving authentic Maine lobster rolls in LA. Barbara Corcoran helped them franchise into a nationwide food truck empire.
currentStatus: Expanded to 30+ franchise locations across the US

name: Tipsy Elves
tagline: Ugly Christmas sweaters and festive apparel for adults who love to party
description: Tipsy Elves creates intentionally ugly Christmas sweaters and fun holiday apparel. Robert Herjavec invested and helped them scale into a multi-million dollar seasonal brand.
currentStatus: Over $125M in lifetime sales

name: Kodiak Cakes
tagline: High-protein pancake and waffle mix made with 100% whole grains
description: Kodiak Cakes makes protein-packed pancake and waffle mixes from whole grains. Despite not getting a deal on Shark Tank, they became one of the top pancake mix brands in America.
currentStatus: No deal taken — grew to $200M+ revenue and went public

name: The Bouqs Co.
tagline: Farm-fresh flowers delivered directly from eco-friendly farms
description: The Bouqs Co. sources flowers directly from sustainable volcanic farms. Kevin O'Leary famously walked out during the pitch, but they later raised $24M and became a major floral brand.
currentStatus: Raised $24M after the show — now a leading online floral brand

name: Grace & Lace
tagline: Lace boot socks and leg warmers that are fashionable and cozy
description: Grace & Lace creates decorative lace boot socks and leg warmers. Barbara Corcoran invested after one of the most emotional pitches in Shark Tank history.
currentStatus: Still operating with millions in annual revenue

name: Drop Stop
tagline: Fills the gap between your car seat and console so things stop falling in
description: Drop Stop is a wedge-shaped car accessory that fills the annoying gap between your seat and center console. Lori Greiner invested and the product became a QVC sensation.
currentStatus: Over $15M in sales, still a bestseller

name: Simple Sugars
tagline: All-natural sugar scrubs safe for sensitive skin, created by a teenager
description: Simple Sugars creates all-natural sugar scrubs designed for sensitive skin. Founded by Lani Lazzari at age 11, she pitched at 18 and received investment from Mark Cuban.
currentStatus: Sales went from $30K to $1M+ in first year post-show

name: ReadeREST
tagline: Magnetic eyeglass holder that clips to your shirt without scratching lenses
description: ReadeREST is a simple magnetic clip that holds reading glasses securely to your shirt. Lori Greiner invested and turned it into a QVC mega-hit.
currentStatus: Over $20M in sales — one of Lori's favorites

name: Wicked Good Cupcakes
tagline: Gourmet cupcakes in a jar, shipped fresh anywhere in the US
description: Wicked Good Cupcakes sells gourmet cupcakes layered in mason jars, perfectly packaged for shipping. Kevin O'Leary invested in a royalty deal.
currentStatus: Multi-million dollar business, ships nationwide

name: Sleep Styler
tagline: Heat-free hair rollers you wear to bed for curls while you sleep
description: Sleep Styler are absorbent, heat-free hair rollers designed to be worn overnight, giving salon-quality curls without heat damage. Lori Greiner invested and helped scale the brand.
currentStatus: Sold over $1M on Amazon in first month post-show

name: The Original Comfy
tagline: A giant wearable blanket hoodie that went viral overnight
description: The Original Comfy is an oversized blanket with sleeves and a hood. It went viral after appearing on Shark Tank and sold out within hours.
currentStatus: Over $150M in sales — viral hit product

name: Night Runner 270
tagline: Shoe-mounted lights that illuminate your path when running at night
description: Night Runner 270 are powerful LED lights that attach to your running shoes, illuminating the ground in front of you for safer nighttime runs.
currentStatus: Still active — popular with runners and outdoor enthusiasts

name: Proof Eyewear
tagline: Sustainable sunglasses made from wood and recycled materials
description: Proof Eyewear makes stylish sunglasses and prescription frames from sustainable materials including wood, recycled aluminum, and cotton-based acetate.
currentStatus: Still independent — built a loyal sustainable lifestyle brand

name: Nerd Wax
tagline: Beeswax that keeps your glasses from sliding down your nose
description: Nerd Wax is a beeswax-based product applied to the bridge of your glasses to keep them from slipping. Despite no deal, the product gained massive exposure.
currentStatus: Still operating — popular affordable glasses accessory

name: BuggyBeds
tagline: Glue-based traps that detect bed bugs before an infestation starts
description: BuggyBeds are small adhesive monitors placed in beds and furniture to detect bed bugs early. Multiple sharks invested — a rare all-in moment.

deal.investor: Barbara Corcoran, Kevin O'Leary, Lori Greiner, Mark Cuban

currentStatus: Expanded internationally — sold in major retailers

name: Breathometer
tagline: The world's first smartphone breathalyzer
description: Breathometer was a pocket-sized breathalyzer that plugged into your smartphone. It received investment from all five sharks — one of the rarest events in Shark Tank history.
currentStatus: All 5 sharks invested — later had FTC issues but a landmark Shark Tank moment

name: Doorman
tagline: Package delivery service that delivers when you're actually home
description: Doorman was an on-demand package delivery service that let customers schedule deliveries for evenings and weekends. Despite a clever pitch, no sharks invested.
currentStatus: Shut down in 2017 due to high operational costs

name: Lovepop
tagline: Intricate 3D pop-up greeting cards engineered with ship-building techniques
description: Lovepop creates stunning 3D pop-up greeting cards using a paper engineering technique inspired by ship architecture. Kevin O'Leary invested and helped them scale massively.
currentStatus: Over $20M in revenue — now a leading greeting card brand

name: Sand Cloud
tagline: Turkish towels and beach accessories that help save marine life
description: Sand Cloud sells premium Turkish towels and beach accessories, donating a portion of profits to marine conservation. Barbara Corcoran invested in the socially-conscious beach brand.
currentStatus: Multi-million dollar brand with strong social media following

name: Fizzics
tagline: Turns any beer into a draft beer experience at home
description: Fizzics is a device that uses fluid dynamics to transform any canned or bottled beer into a micro-foam draft-quality beer. Mark Cuban and Lori Greiner both invested.
currentStatus: Still available — popular gift for beer enthusiasts

name: The Adventure Challenge
tagline: Scratch-off adventure book with mystery date ideas for couples
description: The Adventure Challenge is a scratch-off book with 50 mystery adventures for couples, friends, or families. You scratch to reveal the activity and commit to doing it before seeing the details.
currentStatus: Millions sold — popular gift for couples and families

name: Dude Wipes
tagline: Flushable wipes made specifically for men
description: Dude Wipes are flushable personal hygiene wipes marketed to men. Mark Cuban invested and helped them grow into one of the top personal care brands in the US.
currentStatus: Over $100M in revenue — now in Walmart, Target, and major retailers

name: Simply Fit Board
tagline: The workout board with a twist — balance and core training made fun
description: Simply Fit Board is a balance board that works your core while doing simple twisting motions. Lori Greiner invested and it became one of QVC's best-selling fitness products.
currentStatus: Over $160M in sales — one of the highest-grossing Shark Tank products

name: GrooveBook
tagline: Monthly photo book subscription — 100 photos printed and mailed for $2.99
description: GrooveBook was a subscription service that printed 100 smartphone photos into a perforated photo book and mailed it monthly for $2.99. Mark Cuban and Kevin O'Leary invested. It was later acquired by Shutterfly for $14.5M.
currentStatus: Acquired by Shutterfly for $14.5M — huge exit

name: Spatty Daddy
tagline: Long flexible spatula that gets every last bit out of bottles and jars
description: Spatty Daddy is a long, flexible spatula designed to reach inside bottles, jars, and tubes to get every last drop. Barbara Corcoran invested in the practical kitchen tool.
currentStatus: Still selling — a practical kitchen staple

name: Talbott Teas
tagline: Luxury artisan teas blended by a celebrity stylist
description: Talbott Teas were premium artisan tea blends created by celebrity stylist Shane Talbott. Magic Johnson made a guest appearance and invested.
currentStatus: Acquired by Jamba Juice

name: iON Ball
tagline: Exercise ball with a built-in handle for easier transport and storage
description: iON Ball is an exercise ball with a retractable handle for easy carrying. Despite a reasonable pitch, no sharks invested.
currentStatus: No longer in business

name: Chef Big Shake
tagline: Shrimp burgers and seafood patties as a healthy alternative to beef
description: Chef Big Shake created shrimp-based burger patties as a healthier alternative to beef. Kevin O'Leary invested in the seafood brand.
currentStatus: Expanded into retail — available in grocery stores

name: Pork Barrel BBQ
tagline: Award-winning BBQ sauces and rubs created by two Washington DC lobbyists
description: Pork Barrel BBQ was started by two DC lobbyists who won multiple BBQ championships. Barbara Corcoran invested in their line of sauces and rubs.
currentStatus: Available nationwide in major grocery chains

name: Game Face
tagline: Disposable face shields to protect your skin during sports
description: Game Face makes disposable protective face shields for contact sports. Kevin O'Leary and Robert Herjavec both passed, but Daymond John saw potential.
currentStatus: Discontinued — market proved too niche

name: Berri Fit
tagline: All-natural, low-calorie sports drinks made with coconut water
description: Berri Fit was a line of natural sports drinks made with coconut water and real fruit. Despite a health-forward pitch, no deal was made.
currentStatus: No longer in business

name: Eco Nuts
tagline: Natural soap berries that replace laundry detergent
description: Eco Nuts are dried soapberry shells that naturally produce soap when wet, used as a chemical-free laundry detergent alternative. Robert Herjavec invested in the eco-friendly product.
currentStatus: Still operating — popular in eco-conscious households

name: Chord Buddy
tagline: Guitar learning device that makes playing chords easy for beginners
description: Chord Buddy is a device that attaches to a guitar neck and helps beginners play chords immediately. Robert Herjavec invested in the educational music tool.
currentStatus: Still active — popular beginner guitar accessory

name: Tower Paddle Boards
tagline: Direct-to-consumer paddle boards at half the retail price
description: Tower Paddle Boards sells inflatable stand-up paddle boards directly to consumers, cutting out middlemen to offer premium boards at affordable prices. Mark Cuban invested.
currentStatus: Over $30M in sales — one of Mark Cuban's best early investments

name: Coffee Meets Bagel
tagline: Dating app that sends you one quality match per day
description: Coffee Meets Bagel is a dating app that curates one match per day for quality over quantity. The founders famously turned down Mark Cuban's $30M buyout offer on the show.
currentStatus: Turned down $30M from Mark Cuban — still operating as a dating app

name: Scholly
tagline: App that matches students with college scholarships they qualify for
description: Scholly is an app that helps students find college scholarships they're eligible for. The pitch caused a rare argument between sharks, with Lori and Daymond investing against the others' objections.
currentStatus: Helped students find $100M+ in scholarships

name: Bottle Breacher
tagline: Bottle openers made from decommissioned .50 caliber bullets by veterans
description: Bottle Breacher makes handcrafted bottle openers from decommissioned .50 caliber bullets, made by military veterans and their families. Mark Cuban and Kevin O'Leary both invested.
currentStatus: Over $12M in sales — beloved veteran-owned brand

name: Hold Your Haunches
tagline: Shapewear pants that smooth and sculpt without being uncomfortable
description: Hold Your Haunches makes comfortable shapewear pants designed to smooth and sculpt the figure without the discomfort of traditional shapewear. Lori Greiner invested.
currentStatus: Still operating in the shapewear market

name: Honeyfund
tagline: Crowdfunding honeymoon registry platform for modern couples
description: Honeyfund is a wedding registry platform where couples can register for honeymoon experiences instead of physical gifts. Kevin O'Leary and Lori Greiner invested.
currentStatus: Over $500M in honeymoon funds raised for couples

name: The Lip Bar
tagline: Bold, vegan lipsticks in shades for all skin tones
description: The Lip Bar is a vegan, cruelty-free lipstick brand offering bold shades designed for all skin tones. Despite being rejected by all sharks, the founder grew it into a multi-million dollar brand now sold at Target.
currentStatus: Rejected by all sharks — now sold in 1,200+ Target stores

name: Electric Pickle
tagline: A sports bar concept with pickleball courts built in
description: Electric Pickle is an entertainment concept combining a sports bar with indoor pickleball courts. Mark Cuban invested in the fast-growing pickleball entertainment space.
currentStatus: Expanding nationally — riding the pickleball boom

name: Trunkster
tagline: Smart rolling luggage with a built-in scale, GPS, and USB charger
description: Trunkster was a smart suitcase with a zipperless slide-open design, built-in scale, GPS tracker, and USB charger. Despite being a crowdfunding hit, the company later collapsed.
currentStatus: Company went bankrupt — failed to deliver on promises

name: Copa di Vino
tagline: Single-serve wine by the glass in a sealed cup
description: Copa di Vino sells premium wine by the glass in single-serve sealed cups, perfect for events and outdoor venues. The founder pitched twice and became famous for declining multiple offers.
currentStatus: Grew to $14M in revenue without a deal — later sold the company

name: Mission Belt
tagline: Ratchet belts without holes — a perfect fit every time
description: Mission Belt makes leather belts with a ratchet buckle system instead of holes, allowing a precise fit in quarter-inch increments. Daymond John invested.
currentStatus: Multi-million dollar belt brand available nationwide

name: Happy Feet
tagline: Memory foam slippers with massaging foot insoles
description: Happy Feet are plush slippers with built-in massaging insoles that promote foot health. Multiple sharks competed for the deal.
currentStatus: Still operating — popular comfort slipper brand

name: Shark Wheel
tagline: Cube-shaped skateboard wheels that outperform traditional round wheels
description: Shark Wheel created a sinusoidal (cube-like) skateboard wheel that claims to outperform traditional round wheels in speed, grip, and debris clearance. Mark Cuban invested.
currentStatus: Expanded into luggage and industrial wheels

name: Roominate
tagline: STEM building toy designed to inspire girls in engineering
description: Roominate is a construction toy designed to inspire young girls in STEM fields, featuring buildable dollhouses with real circuitry. Lori Greiner and Kevin O'Leary both invested.
currentStatus: Acquired by PlayMonster — continues as an educational toy

name: Paint Brush Cover
tagline: Airtight covers that keep paint brushes and rollers fresh between coats
description: Paint Brush Cover creates airtight covers that keep wet paint brushes and rollers fresh for days, eliminating the need to clean them between coats. Lori Greiner invested.
currentStatus: Available at major hardware stores and online

name: ezpz
tagline: Silicone mats with built-in plates that suction to any surface
description: ezpz makes silicone placemats with built-in plates and bowls that suction to tables, preventing kids from tipping their food. Lori Greiner invested in the mealtime solution.
currentStatus: Over $20M in revenue — a must-have for parents

name: Manscaped
tagline: Below-the-waist grooming tools designed specifically for men
description: Manscaped makes grooming products specifically designed for men's body grooming, including waterproof trimmers engineered to be safe below the belt.
currentStatus: No deal — grew to $100M+ revenue independently and went public via SPAC

name: NumNum
tagline: Pre-spoon baby utensils designed for self-feeding
description: NumNum GOOtensils are uniquely shaped pre-spoon baby feeding utensils that help babies learn to self-feed. Daymond John invested in the innovative baby product.
currentStatus: Available at Target, Walmart, and baby specialty stores

name: Bare Ease
tagline: Numbing cream kit designed to reduce pain during waxing
description: Bare Ease is a numbing cream specifically formulated to reduce pain during waxing treatments. Lori Greiner saw the potential in the beauty pain-relief market.
currentStatus: Available in salons and online beauty retailers

name: Zipz Shoes
tagline: Shoes with interchangeable uppers — one sole, unlimited styles
description: Zipz Shoes feature a unique zip system allowing you to swap out the upper portion of the shoe, giving you multiple styles from one pair of soles. Kevin O'Leary invested $2.5M in one of the biggest deals in Shark Tank history.
currentStatus: One of the largest deals in Shark Tank history at the time

name: Beer Blizzard
tagline: Frozen disc that keeps canned beer ice cold without dilution
description: Beer Blizzard is a reusable frozen disc that sits under a beer can to keep it cold without the beer getting watered down. No deal was made but the product gained cult following.
currentStatus: Still operating — popular tailgating accessory

name: Beard King
tagline: Cape bib that catches beard trimmings before they hit the sink
description: Beard King is a grooming bib that attaches to a bathroom mirror with suction cups, catching beard trimmings to keep sinks and floors clean. Kevin O'Leary made an offer.
currentStatus: Over $1M in sales — popular men's grooming accessory

name: Kettle Gryp
tagline: Turns any dumbbell into a kettlebell in seconds
description: Kettle Gryp is a clamp-on device that attaches to a dumbbell and turns it into a functional kettlebell, saving gym-goers from having to buy a full set of kettlebells.
currentStatus: Over $5M in sales — popular home gym accessory

name: Bala Bangles
tagline: Stylish weighted wristbands and anklebands for low-impact workouts
description: Bala Bangles are sleek, fashionable weighted bands worn on wrists or ankles to add resistance to everyday activities and workouts. Maria Sharapova and Mark Cuban both invested.
currentStatus: Available at major retailers — $30M+ in sales

name: BottleBright
tagline: All-natural tablets that clean water bottles and hydration packs
description: BottleBright are effervescent cleaning tablets that clean water bottles, hydration packs, and thermoses without chemicals. Kevin O'Leary invested in the eco-friendly cleaning product.
currentStatus: Still available — popular among outdoor and fitness enthusiasts

name: Two Blind Brothers
tagline: Luxury apparel brand founded by two brothers who are legally blind
description: Two Blind Brothers is a clothing brand founded by two brothers with a degenerative eye disease. They donate all profits to fund research for a cure. Robert Herjavec invested.
currentStatus: Donated millions to eye disease research

name: Clever Yoga
tagline: Premium non-slip yoga mats made from sustainable materials
description: Clever Yoga makes premium yoga mats and accessories with excellent grip and eco-friendly materials. Lori Greiner invested in the growing yoga market.
currentStatus: Top-rated yoga mat on Amazon

name: Groomed
tagline: At-home dog grooming subscription box
description: Groomed is a subscription box service delivering professional-grade dog grooming products to pet owners. Daymond John invested in the pet care subscription.
currentStatus: Operating in the growing pet care market

name: I Want to Draw a Cat for You
tagline: Custom hand-drawn cat cartoons mailed to anyone for $9.95
description: A man sold custom hand-drawn cat cartoons for $9.95 each. Mark Cuban invested $25,000 in what became one of the quirkiest pitches in Shark Tank history.
currentStatus: Memorable pitch — became an internet sensation

name: Alarm.com
tagline: Smart home security and automation platform
description: Alarm.com is a smart home security and automation platform. The company appeared early on Shark Tank and has since grown into a publicly traded company worth billions.
currentStatus: Publicly traded — multi-billion dollar smart home platform

name: Numilk
tagline: Machine that makes fresh plant-based milk in 60 seconds
description: Numilk creates machines that produce fresh oat, almond, or other plant-based milks on demand. Mark Cuban and Alex Rodriguez co-invested in the plant milk revolution.
currentStatus: Growing plant-based milk brand in retail locations

name: Yumble
tagline: Healthy, ready-to-eat kids meal delivery service
description: Yumble delivers fresh, healthy, ready-to-eat meals designed for kids. The meals are nutritious, convenient, and kid-approved.
currentStatus: Operating in the kids meal delivery space
